# canShowMIMEType(_:)

**Type Method**

Returns whether the receiver can display content of a given MIME type.

## Declaration

```swift
@MainActor class func canShowMIMEType(_ MIMEType: String!) -> Bool
```

## Parameters

- **MIMEType**: The MIME type of the content.

## Return Value

[doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true] if the receiver can display content of the specified MIME type where `MIMEType` is one of the standard types like “image/gif”; otherwise, [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false].

## Getting and Setting Content Information

- **mimeTypesShownAsHTML()**: Returns a list of MIME types that WebKit renders as HTML.
- **setMIMETypesShownAsHTML(_:)**: Sets the MIME types that WebKit attempts to render as HTML.
- **canShowMIMEType(asHTML:)**: Returns whether the receiver interprets a MIME type as HTML.
- **supportsTextEncoding**: A Boolean that indicates whether the document view supports different text encodings.
- **customTextEncodingName**: The custom text encoding name.
- **textSizeMultiplier**: The font size multiplier for text displayed in web frame view objects managed by the receiver.
